commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the Codex app-server event projector and tests so non-commentary assistant deltas stream through both `onPartialReply` and `stream: "assistant"` agent events, with replacement markers when the assistant item id changes.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +70. Total +81 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main gates Codex partials on `final_answer` while upstream Codex delta notifications do not carry phase, and the PR's replacement path can be traced through live-chat/TUI and Teams consumers without executing a live run.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can preserve the PR's Codex streaming direction while making replacement semantics safe across affected consumers.

target.title: fix(codex): stream non-final-answer assistant deltas as partials
target.body:
Fixes #95422

Codex partial-reply streaming was gated on an assistant item phase of `final_answer`. Newer Codex app-servers (>= 0.139) stream `item/agentMessage/delta` events without tagging that phase mid-turn, so the gate never fired. Even after unblocking `onPartialReply`, TUI/WebChat still showed one chunk because they consume **`stream: "assistant"` agent events** (gateway `emitChatDelta`), and Codex only emitted one terminal assistant event at turn completion.

Stream every non-commentary assistant delta as both `onPartialReply` and an assistant agent event; let turn completion pick the final user-visible message (it already excludes commentary and superseded intermediate items from the persisted reply). Commentary items stay progress-only.

Sibling of #86050 (same symptom on the `claude-cli` runtime; candidate fix #86649).

## What Problem This Solves

Agents running on the Codex app-server runtime (the default for `gpt-5*` models when OpenAI access is via ChatGPT OAuth) produced **no live partial streaming** in the TUI (`openclaw chat`) and WebChat/Control UI. The full answer appeared in a single chunk at turn completion, even though the interactive Codex CLI streams the same turn token-by-token.

Two gaps in `CodexAppServerEventProjector.handleAssistantDelta`:

1. **Phase gate:** only forwarded partials when item phase was `final_answer`. Codex `>= 0.139.0` (OpenClaw manages `0.134.0`) streams deltas with **no phase**, so the gate never fired.
2. **TUI delivery path:** TUI/WebChat stream via gateway `emitChatDelta`, which reacts to **`stream: "assistant"` agent events**, not `onPartialReply`. Codex called `onPartialReply` mid-turn but only emitted one terminal assistant agent event at completion — so control-ui surfaces still got a single chunk.

## Why This Change Was Made

- **Commit 1:** stream all non-commentary deltas through `onPartialReply` (restores channel draft/progress paths blocked by the `final_answer` gate).
- **Commit 2:** also emit `stream: "assistant"` agent events with `{ text, delta }` on each non-commentary delta so TUI/WebChat receive throttled `emitChatDelta` payloads (matches how embedded runtimes and CLI bridges feed live surfaces).

Turn completion still selects the final user-visible message; commentary stays progress-only.

Tradeoff (for reviewers): intermediate non-commentary items can stream live and then be superseded by the final reply (gateway reconciles via `replace`; persisted reply unchanged).

## User Impact

- Restores incremental answer streaming in TUI and WebChat for Codex-runtime agents (`gpt-5*` / ChatGPT-OAuth).
- No change to the persisted/delivered final message; commentary still surfaces as progress only.

## Evidence

**Live TUI validation** (OpenClaw `2026.6.8`, `main` agent, Codex runtime, `codex-cli 0.139.0`):

| Stage | Projector partials | Gateway assistant events | `emitChatDelta` sends | TUI |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Before PR | 0 (gate blocked) | 1 terminal, no delta | 1 full chunk | one chunk |
| After commit 1 only | ~505 | 1 terminal, no delta | 1 full chunk | one chunk |
| After both commits | ~505 | ~100 incremental | ~100 throttled deltas | streaming ✓ |

**Protocol:** `codex exec --json` on 0.139.0 emits answer items with **no `phase` field** mid-stream.

**Automated tests** — `pnpm vitest run extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts` → **96/96 passing**, including `streams assistant deltas when the app-server omits the item phase` (asserts both `onPartialReply` and `onAgentEvent({ stream: "assistant", data: { text, delta } })`).

**Lint** — `npx oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.{ts,test.ts}` → clean.

See [comment with full probe details](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95404#issuecomment-4760189655).
target.author: agonza1

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
